events.title: Free Canoeing on the Gowanus Canal
events.description:
Join the Gowanus Dredgers for free, self-guided canoe trips on the Gowanus Canal. No experience required! Hop into a Gowanus Dredgers canoe and paddle at your own pace inside the protected Gowanus Canal. Enjoy discovering the canal’s unique mix of wildlife and urban waterway infrastructure, practice your paddling skills, or take a guided audio tour and learn more about the history, cleanup and revitalization of our favorite urban waterway.

You’ll be outfitted with a life jacket (which must be worn at all times) and paddle, and receive some basic instruction on how to paddle and safety tips on how to use a canoe, and help you set off on your own self-guided journey. Trips may be limited to 20 minutes if there’s a crowd — but usually they just expect you to return to the dock 15 min. prior to closing.

Free walk-up canoeing is open to all ages. Groups, families, solo paddlers, and intrepid first dates are all welcome! Minors require a guardian’s signature to participate, and all participants must sign a waiver

Dress for the weather: wear sunscreen, hats, and bring a water bottle — and bring layers if there’s a breeze! Close-toed shoes are recommended, and wear clothing you don’t mind potentially being splashed.

See website for complete schedule.
events.recurrence_description: Saturdays and Sundays, various, through Oct. 18;

events.title: The Museum of Modern Art Presents Frida and Diego: The Last Dream
events.description:
The Museum of Modern Art announces an art exhibition, Frida and Diego: The Last Dream.

Frida and Diego: The Last Dream celebrates Frida Kahlo and Diego Rivera—two of Mexico’s most beloved icons of 20th-century art—in a first-of-its-kind collaboration with the Metropolitan Opera. Presented in conjunction with the Met’s new production of El Último Sueño de Frida y Diego (May 14–June 5, 2026), composed by Gabriela Lena Frank with a libretto by Nilo Cruz, the exhibition features artworks by Kahlo and Rivera in an elaborate installation designed by Jon Bausor, the set and co-costume designer of the opera. For both the opera and exhibition, Bausor weaves visual references from the artists’ lives and artworks into his theatrical designs.

Key participants in a movement to redefine Mexican culture and identity after the revolution of 1910–20—Rivera through monumental murals and Kahlo through intimate self-portraits—the artists were romantically involved from 1928 until Kahlo’s death in 1954. The fictional narrative of El Último Sueño de Frida y Diego begins three years after Kahlo’s death and follows an aging Rivera as he summons the deceased Kahlo back to life on the Day of the Dead, a Mexican holiday honoring passed loved ones. As the opera and exhibition design attest, the pair continue to have an enduring influence on artists across the visual and performing arts.
events.cost: Included with admission: $17-$30; free for ages 16 and younger
events.recurrence_description: Saturdays – Thursdays, 10:30 am – 5:30 pm, Fridays, 10:30 am – 8:30 pm, through Sept. 12;

events.title: The Very Hungry Caterpillar Show
events.description: The Very Hungry Caterpillar Show brings four classic Eric Carle stories off-the-page and onto-the-stage, told with 75 Magical Puppets.  Alongside The Very Hungry Caterpillar, your family will journey across the seas with 10 Little Rubber Ducks, search the night with The Very Lonely Firefly, and, as a special treat this summer only, meet the colorful characters of Brown Bear, Brown Bear.
events.recurrence_description: Saturdays, 9:30 am, Saturdays, 11:30 am, Saturdays, 3:30 pm, Sundays, 10 am, Sundays, noon, Sundays, 2 pm, Sundays, 4 pm, Thursdays and Fridays, 11 am, through Aug. 30; except Aug. 20 and 27

events.title: Music Adventure for Toddlers with Treasure Trunk
events.description: A magical musical adventure for little ones at Corlears School! Led by Treasure Trunk’s Mr. Kyle, this imaginative class blends storytelling, costumes, and music to spark movement, connection, and joyful play. Plus, Corlears Admissions will be on hand to share more about our Toddler Program for toddlers ages 10–24 months.

events.title: Walt Whitman Birthplace Museum – Girl Scout Programs!
events.description:
Discover, Create, and Earn Badges at the Walt Whitman Birthplace Museum!
Girl Scouts can step into history, explore the life of Walt Whitman, and express their creativity through fun, hands-on activities. Our badge-earning programs inspire leadership, literacy, and a love of the arts. Book your troop’s visit today for an unforgettable experience!
events.cost: Pricing ranges from $15 to $20 per registered Scout, depending on the program selected.
events.recurrence_description: Saturdays and Sundays, all day, through July 31;

events.title: Eisenhower Park FREE Concert w/ AUDAWIND
events.description:
It’s Country Night at Eisenhower Park! Audawind is bringing their high energy coastal country rock to The Harry Chapin Lakeside Theater on July 18th at 7PM. After our show, Simply Stapleton will take the stage to play all of your favorite Chris Stapleton tunes. This is an outdoor concert, so bring a lawn chair…cowboy hats and boots are highly encouraged!
This is a HUGE FREE show, make sure to get there early and secure your seat!
#longislandmusic #EisenhowerPark #band

events.title: Volunteer with EQA: Community Garden
events.description:
Calling All lovers of Gardening! We Need Your Help!

The Eastern Queens Alliance has created a Community Peace Garden right outside of the Idlewild Environmental Center on an unused street lot. The project is in process and needs volunteers to work with the EQA Gardener. It is our mission to create beautiful spaces that will have positive impacts on our local community at large .

Saturdays between 9 am to 2 pm 

Prior volunteers can redo this form to update their availability for gardening sessions!
events.recurrence_description: Saturdays, 9 am – 2 pm, through July 31;

events.title: Miles for the Future 5K
events.description:
July 18, 2026
8:30AM Start – Rain or Shine

Van Cortlandt Park

Across the street from Lloyd’s Carrot Cake located at 6087 Broadway, Bronx, NY
(LOOK FOR THE TORTOISE & HARE STATUE)

Race day registration and check in will be located near the tortoise and hare statue.

Miles for the Future 5K is proudly hosted by the Nu Chi Chi Chapter of Chi Eta Phi Sorority, Inc. to support the advancement of nursing and service within our community. Proceeds from this event will directly fund our undergraduate and graduate nursing scholarship programs, while also strengthening our chapter’s community service initiatives. Through this race, we invest in the future of healthcare and continue our commitment to service for humanity.

AMENITIES
Medals to all finishers
Registrants recieve a shirt
Refreshments
LIVE / text / email results
FREE finish line photos
Personalized bib if registered 3 weeks in advance

AWARDS
Top 3 F/M/X Overall
